name: Optimization And Domain Boundary Pass

messages.content:
Using `github:yeet` for the commit/push part of the workflow, with the same constraint as before: only real source changes get staged, and local `.cozter` state stays out. I’ll first rescan tracked sources for low-risk optimization candidates, then patch only evidence-backed findings.

The repo is on `main` and still only has `.cozter` local state dirty. I’m going to keep this pass source-only and start with mechanical optimization signals: accidental copies, avoidable allocations, unused/dead helpers, and repeated work in hot-looking paths.

The first pass did not reveal obvious accidental heavy range copies or `return std::move` pessimizations. I’m moving to higher-signal scans now: local vectors built in loops without capacity planning, repeated conversions inside render/asset paths, and private helpers that appear unreferenced.

The alloc-heavy paths are mostly already using scratch arenas and reserved vectors. I’m running a private-helper reference pass now to catch dead local helpers and then I’ll inspect a few repeated block/layout calculations where a small optimization may be defensible.

I found one broadly useful optimization that is safe to apply: replace repeated `(value + divisor - 1) / divisor` ceil-division formulas with a shared overflow-safe `DivideUp` helper, then use it in texture layout and dispatch count paths. This removes duplicated arithmetic and avoids the add-before-divide overflow shape.

`apply_patch` touched the right code but mixed line endings in the edited files, so I’m normalizing those files back to the repository’s CRLF source convention before building.

The optimization patch is now small and mechanical: one helper plus call-site replacements in graphics/renderer/threading paths. I’m checking whitespace/line-ending integrity and then I’ll run the Linux build/test path before committing.

Whitespace and CRLF/EOF checks passed for the touched sources. I’m starting the anchored Linux verification now; because this adds a global helper used by headers and graphics code, I’ll build and run the normal `dbg`, `fin`, and `opt` checks rather than only a narrow target.

The `dbg` build caught a type deduction issue from the AVBOIT replacement: `1ull` and this platform’s `u64` are different unsigned types for `Max`. I’m tightening that call to an explicit `Max<u64>` and rerunning.

`dbg` now builds cleanly after the type fix. I’m continuing with `fin` and `opt`; after that I’ll run the matching CTest configs and the two-root resource cooker smoke.

All three configs build. I’m running the configured test suite for `dbg`, `fin`, and `opt` next; then I’ll do the isolated two-root cooker smoke that catches asset-path/resource regressions.

The full CTest suite passes for all configs. I’m doing the isolated two-root `resource_cooker` smokes now, using separate cache/output directories and the empty `--asset-type ""` path.

Verification is green so far: builds, tests, and the three isolated cooker smokes all passed. I’m doing the final diff/scope check now, then I’ll stage only the source changes, commit, and push `main`.

Committed the source-only optimization as `d2b1867` (`Add shared divide-up helper`). I’m pushing `main` now; the `.cozter` local files are still unstaged and will stay local.

Implemented and pushed `d2b1867 Add shared divide-up helper` to `main`.

Changed [global/algorithm.h](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/global/algorithm.h:54) to add `DivideUp`, then replaced duplicated ceil-division arithmetic in thread chunking, meshlet reserve sizing, Vulkan texture/layout paths, and renderer/deformer dispatch sizing. This removes repeated add-before-divide formulas and avoids that overflow-prone shape.

Verification passed:
- `cmake --build __cmake/build/linux-clang-x64 --config dbg|fin|opt`
- `ctest --test-dir __cmake/build/linux-clang-x64 -C dbg|fin|opt --output-on-failure`
- isolated two-root `resource_cooker` smokes for `dbg`, `fin`, and `opt`

`git push origin main` completed. Only local `.cozter` files remain dirty and were not committed.

I’ll treat this as a standards pass over tracked source, using `.helper/standard.md` as the authority and keeping `.cozter` local state out of scope. I’ll first read the standard and current status, then run mechanical checks before making any edits.

The standard is explicit about file banners, CRLF, exact EOF shape, namespace wrappers, include order, and several API-style rules. I’ve confirmed the only existing dirty files are `.cozter` artifacts, so any source differences from this pass will be isolated.

I have 261 tracked source/shader files outside `3rd_parties`. The first mechanical Git whitespace check is clean, so I’m moving to repo-specific checks that Git won’t catch: banner, CRLF exactness, final separator/EOF, header guards, filename shape, and separator indentation.

The file-shape scan found no violations: all tracked source files passed banner, CRLF, exact EOF, header `#pragma once`, filename, tab, trailing-space, and separator-indent checks. I’m now checking the more semantic style rules where matches need inspection before any edit.

One semantic scan produced a real standards signal: a few file-scope `using namespace` directives are outside `__hidden_*` helper namespaces. The standard only blesses those conveniences inside hidden implementation namespaces, so I’m checking whether they can be moved or replaced without widening changes.

The semantic checks so far are clean or intentionally in wrapper/detail code: direct `std::` container/type usage is concentrated in global aliases, C-runtime calls are behind macros, `UniqueLock` is used for condition-variable waiting, and file-scope `using namespace` hits are inside hidden namespaces. I’m doing one last pass for allocator/container and entrypoint rules before deciding whether any edit is justified.

The allocator/container pass produced many default-allocator vectors, but the inspected non-test cases either own data that outlives the local scope or must satisfy existing default-vector APIs; the scratch-backed hot-path temporary cases already use `ScratchAllocator`. I’m not going to churn test fixtures or persistent state into allocator changes without a clear contract change.

Checked all 261 tracked C/C++/GLSL source files outside `3rd_parties` against `.helper/standard.md`.

No source fixes were needed. Verified:

- CRLF line endings, UTF-8, exact final separator + two newline terminators
- banner and `#pragma once` rules
- no tabs/trailing whitespace or indented separator lines
- `git diff --check` clean
- qualifier spacing like `)const` / `)override`
- `virtual ... override` declarations
- entrypoint/`InitializerGuard` patterns
- include-order candidates
- standard wrapper usage for containers/CRT/console/locks
- allocator/container candidates where actionable

There are no source diffs to commit or push. The only dirty files remain local `.cozter` artifacts, left untouched.
check everything is in right domain. if it's not, move them

I’ll treat this as an architecture/domain-boundary pass, not a broad cleanup. I’ll refresh the repo rules and source layout first, then look for code whose ownership doesn’t match its directory or dependency direction.

The repo is still clean except local `.cozter` files. The written standard is mostly style/API, so I’m deriving “domain” from directory ownership, CMake targets, namespaces, and cross-module include direction rather than guessing from filenames alone.

The dependency graph says core domains are intended to stay generic, while `impl/assets_graphics` owns concrete graphics asset types/cooking and `impl/ecs_graphics` owns runtime ECS/rendering glue. I’m checking both directions now: implementation-specific symbols in core, and generic helpers living down in implementation targets.

The include direction is mostly clean: core/global/logger don’t include `impl`, and implementation modules only depend downward. Two headers stand out because they define bits in both Core and Impl namespaces from an `impl/...` path; I’m inspecting whether those are deliberate registration bridges or misplaced declarations.

I found an additional `loader` target that wasn’t in the first shallow source list. I’m folding that into the domain scan now, because loaders are exactly the kind of place where implementation-specific wiring can legitimately live while still being easy to misplace.

The “Core namespace facade in impl headers” is intentional in this tree: Testbed and loader consume `Core::ECSGraphics`/`Core::ECSUI` as public aliases while the target still links to implementation modules. I’m moving on to mechanical checks for actual ownership violations: top-level dependency inversions, source listed in the wrong CMake target, and names/types that leak into lower-level modules.

A real target-domain issue is showing up in CMake, not in include direction: cook-only files are listed in runtime libraries and compile as empty translation units outside `NWB_COOK`. I’m checking the guarded files now, then I’ll move those source-list entries to the cook targets only if the guards confirm they are cook-domain only.

I’m going to make a small CMake-only move: keep cook-only shader/cooker sources out of runtime libraries and leave them listed only under `nwb_graphics_cook` / `nwb_assets_graphics_cook`. This avoids runtime targets owning empty `#if defined(NWB_COOK)` translation units.

The source moves are limited to two CMake target lists. I’m going to verify the cook targets still own those files, then build the runtime and cook libraries so this doesn’t silently break registration or target dependencies.

The `dbg` build completed and now the runtime libraries build without those cook-only sources; the cook libraries still compile them. I’m doing a second scan for similar guarded-source target leaks and checking the regenerated build graph.

The regenerated compile database no longer lists those files under the runtime object directories; they only appear under cook targets with `NWB_COOK=1`. I’m building the same target set in `fin` and `opt` as well because this CMake change applies across configs.

All three configs build the adjusted runtime/cook library targets. I’m doing one link-level check on `resource_cooker` because it is the main consumer of the cook-only targets.

The `dbg` `resource_cooker` executable links after the target-list cleanup. I’m doing final checks now: diff hygiene, status scope, and a repeat domain scan for the source/target boundaries I changed.

The final scan is clean for the runtime-target leak I fixed, and `git diff --check` is clean. I’m running the targeted assets-graphics test in `dbg` because the cook library owns the moved entries.

Found one domain issue and fixed it: cook-only sources were listed in runtime targets.

Removed these from runtime target source lists, leaving them only in cook targets:
- [core/graphics/CMakeLists.txt](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/core/graphics/CMakeLists.txt:1): `shader_cook.*`, `vulkan_shader_compiler.*`
- [impl/assets_graphics/CMakeLists.txt](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/impl/assets_graphics/CMakeLists.txt:1): `graphics_asset_cooker.*`

Verified:
- Built `nwb_graphics`, `nwb_assets_graphics`, `nwb_graphics_cook`, `nwb_assets_graphics_cook` in `dbg`, `fin`, and `opt`
- Built `nwb_resource_cooker` in `dbg`
- Ran `ctest -C dbg -R '^nwb_assets_graphics_tests$'`
- `git diff --check` clean

Only source changes are the two CMake files. Existing `.cozter` local state is still untouched.

I’ll take this as a focused source cleanup pass: remove code that is provably unused and consolidate clear duplication where it does not change ownership boundaries. I’ll keep the scope evidence-backed, leave `.cozter` alone, and verify with the relevant builds/tests after edits.

The tree already has the domain-boundary CMake edits from the prior pass plus `.cozter` local state; I’ll treat those as existing work and avoid reverting them. Next I’m checking the current source graph against the build files so any removal is backed by references, not filename guesses.

The configured main build already uses `-Wall -Wextra`, and the cook-only CMake split is visible in the active build graph. I found no dedicated static-analysis tool installed, so I’m using compiler-backed rebuilds plus reference scans for declarations, target membership, and repeated helper bodies.

The broad scan is noisy because most helpers are intentionally translation-unit local or public API. I’m narrowing it to things that are mechanically defensible: tracked files not referenced by any target, headers with no include/use sites, duplicate helper implementations, and public declarations with no repository call sites.

I found two clean removals: `global/name_pool.h` is a tracked orphan with no include sites or target membership, and `core/assets/asset_pool.h` is listed in `nwb_assets` but has no repository use sites. I’m removing those and folding a duplicated test binary-overwrite helper into one typed helper.

The patch left the touched test source with mixed line endings, so I’m normalizing that file back to the repository’s CRLF rule before building.

The follow-up reference check showed `asset_handle.h` was only supporting the deleted `AssetPool` and has no remaining repository use sites. I’m removing that dependent orphan too.

CMake regenerated cleanly after the source-list deletions. The targeted debug build is still running, and after that I’ll build the final config because the deduplicated overwrite helper is inside `NWB_FINAL` test code.

The final build is compiling the graphics and assets path now. I’m specifically watching this one because it covers the `NWB_FINAL` branches touched in the test helper.

I’m also running the targeted opt build/test so the touched assets test target is covered across the same debug, final, and optimized configurations without doing a whole-repo rebuild.

Removed the provably unused tracked headers `core/assets/asset_pool.h`, `core/assets/asset_handle.h`, and `global/name_pool.h`, and cleaned their `nwb_assets` source-list entries in [core/assets/CMakeLists.txt](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/core/assets/CMakeLists.txt:11).

Folded the duplicated `OverwriteU32`/`OverwriteU64` body into one typed helper in [assets_graphics_tests.cpp](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/tests/assets_graphics/assets_graphics_tests.cpp:1177).

Verified:
`git diff --check`
`cmake --build __cmake/build/linux-clang-x64 --config dbg|fin|opt --target nwb_assets nwb_assets_graphics_tests`
`ctest --test-dir __cmake/build/linux-clang-x64 -C dbg|fin|opt -R assets_graphics --output-on-failure`

Existing `.cozter` local state and the prior domain CMake edits are still present; I left them untouched.

now check the whole sources for optimizable code and fix them.

if you couldn't find more to fix, commit and push to main

Using `github:yeet` for the commit/push portion, because you explicitly want any resulting source cleanup published to `main`. I’ll keep this optimization pass evidence-backed: scan all tracked sources outside `3rd_parties`, patch only clear wins, verify, then commit/push if there are source changes.

There are already source changes in the worktree from the prior cleanup thread, plus unrelated `.cozter` state. I’m going to inspect those diffs first so I can extend them safely instead of accidentally staging local session files.

The prior deletions look intentional and reference-backed; they were already verified in the earlier thread. I’m extending from there, scanning current tracked source only and excluding `.cozter`, with special attention to allocation patterns, duplicated helper bodies, needless copies, and repeated calculations in hot paths.

Most allocation-heavy paths already reserve or use scratch/custom allocators. I’m now checking for broader cleanup signals that are still defensible across the whole tree: orphaned tracked sources, duplicate local helper implementations, and simple compiler-optimizable patterns that don’t require redesign.

I’m not seeing another defensible source change beyond the existing cleanup: the remaining “orphan” hits are false positives from aggregate/public headers, and the allocation scans show the hot construction paths already reserve or use scratch/custom arenas. I’m moving to verification now, then I’ll stage only source files and push `main` if the checks hold.

The debug build regenerated after the CMake source-list changes and is compiling the affected asset, graphics, cook, and resource-cooker targets. The earlier “premature end of file; recovering” was Ninja repairing its log, not a compile failure.

`fin` is rebuilding the same touched target set now. This config matters because the deduplicated overwrite helper is inside `NWB_FINAL` test code.

The optimized config is building now. After this I’ll run the targeted assets-graphics test in all three configs and do a final status check before committing.

The targeted assets-graphics test passes in `dbg`, `fin`, and `opt`. Since this is going straight to `main`, I’m going one step broader now with full `ctest` for the same three configs before staging.

Full tests pass in all three configs. I’m also running the isolated two-root `resource_cooker` smokes because the diff touches cook target ownership; those use temp output/cache directories and won’t be staged.

The verification set is clean, including full `ctest` and the resource-cooker smokes. I’m going to stage only the seven source changes now, explicitly excluding `.cozter`, then commit and push `main`.

Done. I scanned the tracked sources outside `3rd_parties`/`.cozter` and didn’t find another safe optimization beyond the existing cleanup, so I committed and pushed it to `main`.

Committed `f4cd6be Trim unused sources and cook target entries`.

Changes:
- Removed unused `core/assets/asset_handle.h`, `core/assets/asset_pool.h`, and `global/name_pool.h`.
- Removed cook-only graphics/cooker files from runtime target source lists in [core/graphics/CMakeLists.txt](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/core/graphics/CMakeLists.txt:1) and [impl/assets_graphics/CMakeLists.txt](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/impl/assets_graphics/CMakeLists.txt:1).
- Deduplicated the final-config overwrite helper in [assets_graphics_tests.cpp](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/tests/assets_graphics/assets_graphics_tests.cpp:1177).

Verified:
- `git diff --check`
- Built affected asset/graphics/cook/resource-cooker targets in `dbg`, `fin`, `opt`
- Full `ctest -C dbg`, `ctest -C fin`, `ctest -C opt`
- Two-root `resource_cooker` smokes in `dbg`, `fin`, `opt`

Only local `.cozter` session files remain dirty/untracked; they were not committed.

summary: Completed a repo-wide optimization + standards + domain-boundary pass on `main`, keeping local `.cozter` artifacts out of Git. Added an overflow-safe shared ceil-division helper `DivideUp` in `global/algorithm.h` and replaced repeated `(x + d - 1) / d` call sites across graphics/dispatch/layout code; fixed one template type mismatch (`Max<u64>` vs `1ull`) and normalized CRLF after an `apply_patch` mixed-EOL incident. Verified with full Linux `dbg|fin|opt` builds, `ctest -C dbg|fin|opt`, and isolated two-root `resource_cooker` smokes; committed and pushed `d2b1867 Add shared divide-up helper`. Ran a full `.helper/standard.md` compliance scan over ~261 tracked non-`3rd_parties` sources; no edits required and no diffs to commit. Did a domain/ownership scan and found cook-only sources incorrectly listed in runtime targets; fixed by moving them to cook targets only via `core/graphics/CMakeLists.txt` and `impl/assets_graphics/CMakeLists.txt`, then rebuilt relevant runtime/cook targets across configs and spot-checked `nwb_assets_graphics_tests`. Repo ends clean (source-wise); only `.cozter` remains locally dirty/untracked.
long_term: Application entrypoints and tests must use `NWB_DEFINE_APPLICATION_ENTRY_POINT`, create `NWB::Core::Common::InitializerGuard` before startup/CLI work (nesting-safe), return `-1` on startup failure, ensure any synthetic fallback `argv` is null-terminated, and link targets with `nwb_common` before `nwb_alloc`.

Keep changes minimal-churn and evidence-backed outside `3rd_parties`, obey `.helper/standard.md` and `.gitattributes` (CRLF/EOF), and do not commit local `.cozter` artifacts unless explicitly requested.
Use `ScratchArena`/`ScratchAllocator` for temporary/function-local STL containers, and back longer-lived owned containers with the owning domain’s arena/allocator (avoid default heap).
Prefer `ScopedLock` for purely scoped locking and reserve `UniqueLock` for wait/unlock patterns (e.g., condition variables).
Logger global registration must use RAII guards from `logger/client/logger.h` and `logger/server/server.h`, and those guard types must remain `final`, non-copyable, and non-movable.
Preserve external-domain text identifiers exactly end-to-end (including cache keys), treating hashes as derived data.

Renderer stays material-driven: `asset.parameters` is `HashMap<CompactString, CompactString>`, renderer packs typed values into a generic GPU parameter buffer, shaders look up parameters by exact text key hashed to IDs, and transparent rendering uses AVBOIT with the same BXDF lighting helpers as opaque shading.

Asset-system invariants: reject virtual-path override metadata (`Core::Assets::RejectVirtualPathOverrideField`), keep `AssetRef<T>` strongly typed (no raw-text convenience construction), and keep filesystem segment transfer split into typed helpers with cleanup guards.

Math uses column-vector semantics (`M * v`) with translation in `r[3]`, and camera validity requires finite transforms (including scale), a unit finite quaternion rotation, and valid projection data with fallback to the first valid camera.
ECS invariant: `Core::ECS::World::view<T...>()` is read-only enumeration and must not allocate missing component pools, and `View::candidateCount()` is the non-allocating size hint.

Deformable invariants: strongly typed codecs; reject malformed/degenerate/non-finite data; deterministic rebuild from clean base + replay with stable edit IDs; shared tangent-frame rebuild via `core/geometry/tangent_frame_rebuild.*`; debug snapshots/draws use semantically tagged primitives.

Deformable character cooking/import is native `.nwb` only (reject external `asset.source`), validate skin joints vs skeleton size, normalize finite non-negative skin weights, support LBS and DQ (DQ requires rigid transforms with precomputed payloads), and regenerate missing normals/tangents when omitted.

Backward-compatibility cleanup is intentional; do not reintroduce removed compatibility surfaces including legacy deformable surface edit state deserialization, legacy volume-segment naming, the singular `IDevice::executeCommandList` path, or the removed global `Mutex` compatibility alias.

Linux convergence verification is anchored at `__cmake/build/linux-clang-x64` using `ctest -C dbg|fin|opt` plus isolated two-root `resource_cooker` smokes (binaries under `__exec/linux/x64/full/<config>/resource_cooker`) cooking `impl/assets` and `Testbed/assets` together with `--asset-type ""`, and the window-capture smoke may be skipped headless when `XOpenDisplay` fails.
Volume write APIs must accept raw byte ranges and allocator-generic `Vector<u8, Alloc>` wrappers so scratch-backed staging buffers can be used without forcing the default allocator.

Hole/CSG surface edits must generate a bottom cap (not only side walls), with cap triangulation robust to collinear boundary points and commit-time rejection for cases (e.g., tetra “hard-face” adjacency) that would generate intersecting faces.
The Testbed window-capture smoke should commit previews by clicking the `Commit Preview` UI button (not by pressing Enter), since Enter no longer triggers commit in the UI.

`core/graphics/shader_stage_names.h` should only own generic shader stage mapping; renderer/material mesh-compute emulation stage constants belong in the assets/renderer material domain (e.g., `impl/assets_graphics/material_shader_stage_names.h`).
Prefer the shared overflow-safe `DivideUp` helper over ad-hoc ceil-division formulas to avoid add-before-divide overflow and reduce duplication.
Cook-only translation units guarded by `NWB_COOK` should live only in cook targets, not in runtime library source lists.
